message:
fix: harden recovery, reservation release semantics, and token persistence

Four targeted fixes across the agent mail crate stack:

integrity.rs — WAL/SHM copy failures during vacuum recovery are now
logged instead of silently discarded. A missing WAL means recent writes
are lost, so the warning helps operators diagnose data loss scenarios
during recovery. SHM failures are logged at debug level since they are
non-critical.

guard/lib.rs — Fix fail-open bugs in released_ts_marks_record_released():
- is_none_or → is_some_and for Number values: previously, a None f64
  parse (NaN/Infinity) was treated as "released", now correctly treated
  as "not released" (fail-closed)
- Unparseable numeric strings now return false instead of true
- Unexpected JSON types (Bool, Array, Object) now return false instead
  of true, preventing unknown reservation states from being silently
  treated as released

storage/lib.rs — Formatting fix for max_attachment_bytes chained method
call (no behavioral change).

identity.rs — Registration token persistence hardening: when the DB
write for a registration token fails, the token is now cleared and NOT
cached in the in-memory AgentRow. Previously, a failed DB write would
still populate the cache with a token that doesn't exist in storage,
causing sender_token verification in send_message to silently accept
unverifiable identities. Applied to both register_agent and the update
path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
